description_long: This is a pair of gloves made from a light tan or beige fabric, featuring four distinct finger sections with frayed and uneven edges, suggesting wear or intentional design. The back of the gloves displays a rectangular patch with a colorful, painted illustration depicting two figures and a cross, framed by a thin border. The material appears to be a soft, flexible textile, possibly leather or heavy cloth, with visible stitching along the seams. The gloves have a somewhat aged or vintage appearance, with some discoloration and roughness along the edges. There are no visible closure mechanisms such as buttons or straps, indicating a slip-on design. The overall look is utilitarian but decorated, possibly ceremonial or symbolic in nature.
description: A pair of tan, fabric gloves with four finger sections and a painted illustration of two figures and a cross on the back, featuring frayed edges and visible stitching.
description_view_0: Front view showing the painted illustration and the four finger sections with frayed tips.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thinness of the gloves and the length of the finger sections.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the gloves and the illustration from a different angle, with visible seams.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the glove's flatness and the uneven, worn edges of the fingers.
attribute_pattern: illustrated patch with figures and cross
